rationale.R1.1: The draft opens and closes with a contribution claim and gap framing; meaningful improvement to the draft plausibly requires sharpening those elements.
rationale.R1.2: The artifact is a technical causal-inference passage whose core is an identification chain (AIPW, unconfoundedness, overlap, Rosenbaum bounds); technical improvement centrally implicates logical coherence of that chain.
rationale.R1.6: 'Improve this draft' most naturally includes prose clarity, flow, and sentence-level quality, making writing polish a central expected responsibility.

parsed.clarification_question: Should the improvement focus primarily on prose quality and clarity (writing polish), rather than on the technical and logical soundness of the causal identification and estimation argument?
